source.notes: Discovered via pitstop-check@0.1.1. PR #7246 (Jul 2025) added Retry-After support capped at 60s. Gap remains: error types don't expose header to callers, and >60s Retry-After (quota exhaustion / STOP signal) falls back to exponential backoff instead of being classified correctly.

hazard.summary: Vercel AI SDK respects Retry-After only up to 60s — values above 60s fall back to exponential backoff, misclassifying quota exhaustion (STOP) as transient rate limit (WAIT)
signals: PR #7246 capped Retry-After respect at 60s — Anthropic returns Retry-After: 600 for quota exhaustion
Issue #5018 open since Feb 2025 — partial fix merged Jul 2025, gap remains

constraints: Retry-After values above 60s must be classified as STOP or quota exhaustion, not retried with exponential backoff
Error types must expose Retry-After header value to callers implementing custom retry logic
CAP (concurrency/quota limit) and WAIT (transient burst) must be distinguished at the error classification layer
knobs: retryAfterCap — configurable ceiling before escalating to STOP classification
retryAfterMs — exposed on error type for caller consumption
maxElapsedMs — hard cap on total retry window regardless of header value
verification: Run pitstop-check against packages/gateway/src and packages/provider/src/errors — should return zero warnings after fix
Send request that returns Retry-After: 600 — verify SDK classifies as STOP not WAIT
Verify error type exposes retry_after_ms field to callers
